message.content:
Explore this Next.js portfolio project to understand:
1. How the project is structured (src/app, components, etc.)
2. How certifications are currently displayed (if at all)
3. What data files exist (like content.tsx, data files)
4. What images exist in public/images/Certification/
5. The current page layout and any existing certification section

Be thorough - check src/app/page.tsx, src/data/, src/components/, and public/images/. Report file paths, key sections, and how content is organized.

content.thinking: The user wants me to explore a Next.js portfolio project to understand its structure, how certifications are displayed, data files, images, and page layout. Let me start by exploring the project structure broadly.

content.text: I'll explore this Next.js portfolio project thoroughly. Let me start by examining the overall structure and key files in parallel.

content.thinking: Let me now read the remaining key components to complete the picture - the CertificationModal, types, and other important files.

content.text: Now let me read the remaining critical files in parallel.

content.thinking: I now have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me compile my findings into a thorough report.

content.text:
Here is a thorough report of the project structure, certification display, data files, images, and page layout.

---

## 1. Project Structure

The project is a Next.js App Router portfolio for "Carlo C. Baclao". The top-level layout:

```
C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\
  src\
    app\
      page.tsx              -- Main (and only) page
      layout.tsx            -- Root layout (Inter font, dark mode init script)
      api/github/contributions/route.ts  -- API route for GitHub contributions
    components\
      index.ts              -- Barrel export for all components
      Sidebar.tsx           -- Left sidebar (UserProfile + NavigationMenu + ThemeToggle)
      UserProfile.tsx       -- Profile card in sidebar
      NavigationMenu.tsx    -- Section nav links
      ContentArea.tsx       -- Main scrollable content area with typing greeting + scroll progress
      RightSidebar.tsx      -- Right sidebar (GitHub stats, etc.)
      ResumeModal.tsx       -- Full resume modal overlay
      CertificationModal.tsx -- Certification detail modal overlay
      ProjectShowcase.tsx   -- Project card component
      GitHubSection.tsx     -- GitHub activity section
      ParticleBackground.tsx-- Animated particle background (lazy loaded)
      CursorGlow.tsx        -- Cursor glow effect
      LoadingScreen.tsx     -- Initial loading screen
      SwipeHint.tsx         -- Mobile swipe hint
      ThemeToggle.tsx       -- Dark/light mode toggle
      DarkModeToggle.tsx    -- (alternate dark mode component)
      BrandIcons.tsx        -- Social media brand icons (GitHub, LinkedIn, Instagram, TikTok)
      animations\index.tsx  -- Animation primitives (FadeIn, StaggerContainer, CountUp, TiltCard, MagneticButton, RippleButton, etc.)
    contexts\
      index.ts              -- Barrel export for contexts
      NavigationContext.tsx  -- Global nav state (current section, resume modal, cert modal)
      ThemeContext.tsx       -- Theme (dark/light) context
    data\
      content.tsx           -- All section content rendered as JSX (home, about, skills, experience, projects, github, contact)
      resume.ts             -- Structured resume data (personal info, experience, education, skills, certifications)
    hooks\
      useMousePosition.ts
      useReducedMotion.ts
    types\
      index.ts              -- Core type definitions (NavigationSection, CertificationItem, ResumeData, etc.)
      components.ts         -- Component prop interfaces
      context.ts            -- (additional type definitions)
```

---

## 2. How Certifications Are Currently Displayed

Certifications appear in **two places**:

### A. Within the "Experience" section (`content.tsx`, lines 526-574)

The `ExperienceSection` component (rendered when navigating to the "experience" tab) includes a **Certifications** subsection at the bottom, after Work Experience and Education. It:

- Shows a header: `Certifications (15)` using the certification count from `resumeData.certifications`
- Renders a **2-column grid** (`grid grid-cols-2 gap-3`) of clickable cards
- Each card is a `<motion.button>` that calls `openCertModal(cert)` on click
- If `cert.imageUrl` exists: renders a thumbnail image with `next/image`, an overlay gradient, and the cert name/issuer at the bottom
- If no `cert.imageUrl`: renders a fallback with a CheckCircle icon, name, and issuer
- Cards have hover effects (border color change to orange, slight translate up, shadow)

### B. CertificationModal (`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\components\CertificationModal.tsx`)

When a certification card is clicked, a modal opens showing:
- A header with an Award icon and "Certification / Credential Details"
- The certificate image (if `imageUrl` is set) rendered in a 16:10 aspect ratio container with `object-contain`
- The certification name as a bold heading
- Issuer (Building2 icon), Issue Date (Calendar icon), and optionally Credential ID (Award icon)
- ESC key and backdrop click close the modal

### C. Navigation state for certifications

The certification modal state is managed in `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\contexts\NavigationContext.tsx`:
- `selectedCertification: CertificationItem | null`
- `isCertModalOpen: boolean`
- `openCertModal(cert)` and `closeCertModal()` functions

The modal is rendered in `page.tsx` (lines 202-210) conditionally on both `isCertModalOpen` and `selectedCertification` being non-null.

---

## 3. Data Files

### `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\data\resume.ts`

Contains the `resumeData` object with this structure:
- `personalInfo` -- name, title, email, phone, location, linkedin, github
- `summary` -- bio paragraph
- `experience` -- array of 2 `ExperienceItem` objects (AMS project + Data Encoder job)
- `education` -- array of 2 `EducationItem` objects (QC University BS IT, AICS Senior High)
- `skills` -- array of 4 `SkillCategory` objects (Languages, Frameworks & Libraries, Tools & Databases, Platforms & Systems)
- `certifications` -- array of **15 `CertificationItem` objects**

### `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\data\content.tsx`

Exports `getSectionContent()` which returns a `Record<NavigationSection, SectionContent>` with keys: `home`, `about`, `skills`, `experience`, `projects`, `github`, `contact`. Each entry has a `title` string and a `content` ReactNode. The `experience` section delegates to the `ExperienceSection` component which renders work experience, education, and certifications.

### `CertificationItem` type (`C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\src\types\index.ts`, lines 77-86)

```typescript
export interface CertificationItem {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  issuer: string;
  issueDate: Date;
  expiryDate?: Date;
  credentialId?: string;
  credentialUrl?: string;
  imageUrl?: string;
}
```

---

## 4. Images in `public/images/Certification/`

**19 files** exist in `C:\codes\Clean-Portfolio\public\images\Certification\`:

| File | Type |
|------|------|
| Build 13 Projects with PHP MySQL Bootstrap and PDO.jpg | JPG |
| C++ And Java Training Crash Course for Beginners.jpg | JPG |
| Dart & Flutter  The Complete Flutter Development Course.jpg | JPG |
| GIT, GitLab, GitHub Fundamentals for Software Developers.jpg | JPG |
| Getting Started in Cybersecurity 3.0 Self-Paced.pdf | PDF |
| Introduction to Software Engineering.jpg | JPG |
| Introduction to the Threat Landscape 3.0 Self-Paced.pdf | PDF |
| Java Training Crash Course for Java Beginners.jpg | JPG |
| JavaScript Fundamentals to Advanced- Full Stack Development.jpg | JPG |
| Legacy Responsive Web Design V8 Certification.png | PNG |
| Mastering C++ Language - C++ Programming For Beginners.jpg | JPG |
| MongoDB - The Complete MongoDB Developers Course.jpg | JPG |
| NSE 1 Network Security Associate.pdf | PDF |
| NSE 2 Network Security Associate.pdf | PDF |
| Professional Certificate in Project Management.jpg | JPG |
| Python Complete Course with 30+ Hands on Tasks and Solution.jpg | JPG |
| Python Programming The Complete Course for Success.jpg | JPG |
| Technical Introduction to Cybersecurity 3.0 Self-Paced.pdf | PDF |
| Web3  Blockchain Project Manager Certification Course.jpg | JPG |

**Critical observation**: There are 4 PDF files and 15 image files (13 JPG + 1 PNG + 1 legacy Web Design PNG). However, in `resume.ts`, **every single certification entry points its `imageUrl` to the same placeholder file**: `/images/Certification/Dart & Flutter The Complete Flutter Development Course.jpg`. None of the other 18 certification images in the folder are actually referenced by any data entry. This means the certification images need to be mapped correctly to their respective entries.

---

## 5. Current Page Layout

The app uses a **three-column layout** (in `page.tsx`):

```
+------------------+---------------------------+------------------+
|   Left Sidebar   |      Main Content         |  Right Sidebar   |
|   (md:block)     |    (flex-1, scrollable)   |   (lg:block)     |
|                  |                           |                  |
| UserProfile      | ContentArea               | GitHub stats     |
| NavigationMenu   |  - Typing greeting        | (RightSidebar)   |
| ThemeToggle      |  - Scroll progress bar    |                  |
| Footer           |  - Section content        |                  |
+------------------+---------------------------+------------------+
```

- **Left Sidebar** (72 width, `w-72`): Visible on `md+` screens. On mobile, it becomes a swipe-in drawer from the left edge.
- **Main Content Area** (`flex-1`): Always visible. Contains a `ContentArea` component that renders a time-based typing greeting ("GOOD MORNING/AFTERNOON/EVENING!") and the current section's content. Has a scroll progress bar at the top.
- **Right Sidebar**: Visible on `lg+` screens. On smaller screens, it becomes a swipe-in drawer from the right edge.
- **Resume Modal**: Opens as a full-screen overlay when triggered from the sidebar's "View Resume" button.
- **Certification Modal**: Opens as a centered modal overlay when any certification card in the experience section is clicked.

The entire layout is wrapped in `ThemeProvider` > `NavigationProvider`, with a loading screen, particle background, cursor glow effect, grain overlay, and swipe hints for mobile.
